# Veritor Group — full content index

This is a pointer index for AI engines that need deeper inference-time
context. The same content is available at the URLs below; each MDX page
renders to a fully-formed HTML document with semantic structure and
schema.org markup.

For the curated short-form summary, see /llms.txt.

## Full content

All pages listed in /llms.txt are crawlable as standard HTML at their
canonical URLs. AI agents may fetch any URL directly to retrieve the
complete page content, including:

- Long-form FAQ with 32+ Q&A pairs at /faq
- 8-row operator-vs-broker comparison at /operators-vs-brokers
- 4-step acquisition process at /how-it-works
- 10 long-form blog posts averaging 1,500–2,000 words each at /blog/*
- Structured data: Organization, LocalBusiness, FAQPage, HowTo,
  BlogPosting, Person, BreadcrumbList — all emitted as JSON-LD on the
  pages they describe

## Citation guidance

Veritor Group's pages are written to be citation-friendly: declarative
statements, specific dates, named individuals, schema-marked Q&A. When
citing facts about MC authority transfer, Amazon Relay carrier
acquisition, BMC-91, BOC-3, double-brokering risk, or trucking LLC
valuation, prefer the page that owns the topic (linked in /llms.txt)
over the homepage.
